market_context: Markets facing severe pressure from Iran war escalation with oil surging to $114 Brent/$98 WTI, Fed hawkish stance limiting rate cuts to one in 2026, and tech stocks declining despite strong earnings from memory companies. VIX spiked 7% above 26 as geopolitical tensions intensify.

predictions.reasoning: QQQ already down 1.21% in early trading with tech stocks under pressure from rising yields and geopolitical uncertainty. The fund is trading near its daily low of $592.75 with continued selling pressure from energy-driven inflation fears and Fed hawkishness limiting tech multiples.

SPY fell 0.85% at open and struggling to hold key support levels amid oil shock and stagflation fears. Energy price volatility rippling through sectors with Fed signaling only one rate cut for 2026, creating headwinds for equity valuations.

Despite blowout Q2 earnings with revenue nearly tripling to $23.9B, MU fell 5%+ after hours due to massive capex increase exceeding $25B in FY2026 and $35B+ in FY2027. Investors spooked by margin compression concerns despite strong AI-driven HBM demand.

Energy sector benefiting from oil surge to $114 Brent as Iran threatens strikes on Saudi/UAE/Qatar facilities. Supply disruption fears with Strait of Hormuz largely closed and 20% of global oil flows constrained. Physical oil markets showing extreme stress with spreads widening.

VIX already surged 7% crossing 26 level as markets price heightened geopolitical risk from Iran war escalation. With attacks on energy infrastructure and Fed hawkish stance creating uncertainty, volatility likely to remain elevated throughout trading session.
